audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with Ezekiel little-scroll imagery; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for mighty messenger, seven thunders sealed, no more delay, little scroll eaten sweet/bitter.

verses.restored: And I saw another strong messenger descending from heaven, clothed with cloud, rainbow upon head, face like sun, feet like pillars of fire.
He had in hand a little scroll opened, and set his right foot on sea and left on earth.
He cried with great voice as lion roars; when he cried, the seven thunders spoke their voices.
When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, and I heard voice from heaven saying: Seal what the seven thunders spoke, and do not write these things.
The messenger whom I saw standing on sea and earth raised his right hand to heaven,
and swore by the One living into ages of ages, who created heaven and things in it, earth and things in it, sea and things in it, that delay will be no longer,
but in days of voice of seventh messenger, whenever he is about to trumpet, the mystery of the Divine is completed, as he good-newsed to his servants the prophets.
The voice that I heard from heaven again spoke with me, saying: Go, take the opened scroll in hand of messenger standing on sea and earth.
I went to the messenger, saying to him to give me the little scroll. And he says: Take and eat it; it will make your belly bitter, but in your mouth it will be sweet like honey.
I took the little scroll from the messenger's hand and ate it; in my mouth it was sweet like honey, and when I ate it, my belly was made bitter.
And they say to me: It is necessary for you again to prophesy concerning many peoples and nations and tongues and kings.

translation_notes: Little scroll echoes Ezekiel: prophetic word is sweet to receive and bitter to embody.
Seven thunders are sealed; not all revelation is disclosed.
No more delay concerns completion of Divine mystery, not prediction arithmetic.
safety_note: Do not speculate on sealed thunders or use the little-scroll scene for esoteric certainty; the text itself withholds content.
